file.template.vault_ssh_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Authenticate to Vault using AppRole
VAULT_TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST \
  "${VAULT_ADDR}/v1/auth/approle/login" \
  -H "X-Vault-Namespace: ${VAULT_NAMESPACE}" \
  -d "{\"role_id\":\"${VAULT_ROLE_ID}\",\"secret_id\":\"${VAULT_SECRET_ID}\"}" \
  | jq -r '.auth.client_token')

if [ -z "$VAULT_TOKEN" ] || [ "$VAULT_TOKEN" = "null" ]; then
  echo "Error: Failed to authenticate to Vault" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Create temporary directory for SSH keys
SSH_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
trap "rm -rf $SSH_DIR" EXIT

# Write public key to temp file
echo "{{ ssh_public_key }}" > "${SSH_DIR}/id_rsa.pub"

# Sign the public key with Vault
SIGNED_CERT=$(curl -s -X POST \
  "${VAULT_ADDR}/v1/ssh/sign/${VAULT_SSH_ROLE}" \
  -H "X-Vault-Token: ${VAULT_TOKEN}" \
  -H "X-Vault-Namespace: ${VAULT_NAMESPACE}" \
  -d "{\"public_key\":\"$(cat ${SSH_DIR}/id_rsa.pub)\",\"valid_principals\":\"${SSH_USERNAME}\"}" \
  | jq -r '.data.signed_key')

if [ -z "$SIGNED_CERT" ] || [ "$SIGNED_CERT" = "null" ]; then
  echo "Error: Failed to sign SSH key with Vault" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Write signed certificate
echo "$SIGNED_CERT" > /tmp/vault_ssh_key-cert.pub

# Copy private key (must exist on AAP server)
cp ~/.ssh/id_rsa /tmp/vault_ssh_key
chmod 600 /tmp/vault_ssh_key

echo "SSH certificate signed successfully (TTL: 10 minutes)" >&2
